examples.title: Completed (terminal state)
examples.description: Passing `currentStep={steps.length}` advances past the final step, marking every step complete — the terminal "done" state shown after a flow finishes.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Steps } from '@lostgradient/cinder/steps';
  import type { StepItem } from '@lostgradient/cinder/steps';

  const steps: StepItem[] = [
    { id: 'account', label: 'Account', description: 'Create your login' },
    { id: 'profile', label: 'Profile', description: 'Add your details' },
    { id: 'review', label: 'Review', description: 'Confirm and submit' },
  ];
</script>

<Steps {steps} currentStep={steps.length} label="Onboarding" />

examples.title: Interactive steps
examples.description: A step becomes navigable when its `StepItem` carries an `href` (renders the body as a link) or an `onclick` (renders it as a button). The marker and connector stay decorative — only the body region is the focusable, clickable target.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Steps } from '@lostgradient/cinder/steps';
  import type { StepItem } from '@lostgradient/cinder/steps';

  let current = $state(1);

  const steps: StepItem[] = [
    { id: 'account', label: 'Account', description: 'Create your login', href: '#account' },
    {
      id: 'profile',
      label: 'Profile',
      description: 'Jump back to edit',
      onclick: () => (current = 1),
    },
    { id: 'review', label: 'Review', description: 'Confirm and submit' },
  ];
</script>

<Steps {steps} currentStep={current} label="Onboarding" />

examples.title: Skipped step
examples.description: Use `state: "skipped"` when a flow advances past a step without completing it.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Steps } from '@lostgradient/cinder/steps';
  import type { StepItem } from '@lostgradient/cinder/steps';

  const steps: StepItem[] = [
    { id: 'account', label: 'Account', description: 'Create your login' },
    {
      id: 'profile',
      label: 'Profile',
      description: 'Optional details were skipped',
      state: 'skipped',
    },
    { id: 'review', label: 'Review', description: 'Confirm and submit' },
  ];
</script>

<Steps {steps} currentStep={2} label="Onboarding" />

examples.title: Vertical orientation
examples.description: Set `orientation="vertical"` to stack the steps top-to-bottom with the connector running down the side — useful in narrow side panels or wizards.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Steps } from '@lostgradient/cinder/steps';
  import type { StepItem } from '@lostgradient/cinder/steps';

  const steps: StepItem[] = [
    { id: 'account', label: 'Account', description: 'Create your login' },
    { id: 'profile', label: 'Profile', description: 'Add your details' },
    { id: 'review', label: 'Review', description: 'Confirm and submit' },
  ];
</script>

<Steps {steps} currentStep={1} orientation="vertical" label="Onboarding" />
